title: Sponge sauce dumplings
excerpt: Ingredients 500 ml milk 4 whole cardamom pods, bruised 2 whole star anise 2 whole cinnamon sticks 30 ml cornflour 30 ml cake flour 15 ml custard powder extra milk for the dry ingredients 1 ml Salt 2 eggs, separated 5 ml vanilla essence 60 ml melted butter cinnamon sugar Method Place the milk, cardamom, star anise and …
html:
Ingredients

  • 500 ml milk
  • 4 whole cardamom pods, bruised
  • 2 whole pieces star anise
  • 2 whole cinnamon sticks
  • 30 ml cornflour
  • 30 ml cake flour
  • 15 ml custard powder
  • extra milk for the dry ingredients
  • 1 ml Salt
  • 2 eggs, separated
  • 5 ml vanilla essence
  • 60 ml melted butter
  • cinnamon sugar

Method

Place the milk, cardamom, star anise and cinnamon sticks in a pot. Heat to boiling point.

Meanwhile, mix the cornflour, cake flour, custard powder and salt with enough of the extra milk until smooth.

Once the milk starts to boil, slowly stir in the paste. Cook the mixture for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until it thickens. Be careful that it doesn't burn.

Remove the whole spices and set aside for garnishing.

Whisk the egg yolks and gradually stir them into the boiling mixture. Add the essence. Remove from the stove.

Whisk the egg whites stiff and fold them in.

Spoon spoonfuls of the mixture into a greased dish and spoon melted butter and cinnamon sugar over each dumpling.

Garnish with the whole spices and serve warm.

Easy!
